response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer, but the profile does not match this embedded networking security role. The core requirements — strong C for UNIX development, Linux kernel analysis and modification, L2/L3 switch and security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X), and native-level Japanese — are absent or only marginally touched. C/C++ exposure is limited to a short stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, and there is no evidence of networking-security, firmware, or kernel-level work.
strengths: Long, varied software engineering career with strong testing and craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
Some systems-level experience: low-latency DLang services, C/C++ IDE components, Linux familiarity
Demonstrated ownership and open-source contributions across multiple domains
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency, which is a hard requirement
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, or security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF)
Minimal recent C programming and no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience

key_factors.factor: General seniority and systems programming exposure
